rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative 2,1-difunctionalization (azolation/hydroxyazolation, diamination, oxoamination, fluoroamination) of alkenes using N-mesyl-1,2,3-triazole as triazole radical precursor and various nucleophiles
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of in situ generated NH-1,2,3-triazole (from hydrolysis of N-mesyl-triazole) to an N-centered triazolyl radical; concomitant cathodic reduction of protons evolves H2; the relayed C-radical is further anodically oxidized to a carbocation

key_intermediates.name: C-centered benzylic radical E (after addition to alkene)
benzylic carbocation F (after second anodic oxidation)
alkene radical cation (E', Path A, disfavored)

electron_flow: N-mesyl-triazole hydrolyzes to NH-triazole (2H-tautomer dominant); the anode oxidizes the deprotonated triazole to an N-centered radical that adds to the alkene, generating a benzylic C-radical that is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation, which is trapped by the nucleophile (H2O, ROH, NH3, AcOH, Py·HF); protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: anodic oxidation of NH-1,2,3-triazole to the N-centered radical (CV onset ~2.18 V vs SCE, matching anode potential ~2.25 V)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed; no desired product formed

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; BHT-triazolyl adduct 53 isolated in 76% yield, confirming triazole N-radical

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signals (AN1=13.23 G, AN2=4.58 G, AH=14.70 G, g=2.0060) consistent with N-centered triazolyl radical spin adduct 53a

probe_reagents.name: (E)- and (Z)-β-methylstyrene 54
probe_reagents.observation: Both stereoisomers gave product 55 with identical dr ~1:1, indicating a free carbocation intermediate trapped by water

probe_reagents.name: 1H-1,2,3-triazole 56 / 2H-1,2,3-triazole 57
probe_reagents.observation: Both gave N2-selective product 4 in 81% and 78% yield, confirming NH-triazole as the key intermediate and 2H-tautomer as the productive form

probe_reagents.name: Michael addition test on alkenyl product 47
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed with any nucleophile, excluding nucleophile-first radical pathway

notes: Two pathways were considered (Fig. 6): Path A invokes alkene radical cation (E') as the initial oxidized species followed by triazole radical/nucleophile addition; this is disfavored because alkene 2 has a higher oxidation potential than NH-triazole. Path B (favored) starts with anodic oxidation of NH-triazole to N-centered radical, addition to alkene, second anodic oxidation of the C-radical to a carbocation, and nucleophilic trapping. The 2H-tautomer (B') is responsible for the high N2-regioselectivity via steric effects. Reaction is highly sensitive to potential window (2.5 V optimal; <2.2 V gives only trace, >2.6 V gives decomposition on Pt).
